content:
# Dynamic Retention

**Summary**: The amount of chemical substance retained by the rock during a flow process.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2024-al-azani|Al-Azani, 2024]] evaluated dynamic retention to balance oil recovery with surfactant loss.

## Scientific Context

- [[2024-al-azani|Al-Azani, 2024]] High dynamic retention can lead to high chemical costs and reduced effectiveness in the reservoir.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2024-al-azani|Al-Azani, 2024]] determined via HPLC analysis of produced effluent.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Dynamic retention for GS in seawater was 0.37 mg/g-rock on an outcrop plug. ([[2024-al-azani|Al-Azani, 2024, p. 24]])
